name: GitHub
one_liner: GitHub's public API allows developers to create integrations, retrieve data, and automate workflows across repositories, issues, pull requests, and other GitHub platform features.

evidence.claim: The GitHub REST API is fully described in an OpenAPI compliant document.
The GitHub REST API provides programmatic access to repositories, issues, pull requests, users, and webhooks.
Most endpoints use Authorization: Bearer and Accept: application/vnd.github+json headers.

auth_notes: Authentication is primarily handled via Bearer tokens using GitHub Apps (installation tokens) or OAuth Apps (user access tokens), and Personal Access Tokens.
mcp_notes: No official or well-known community MCP server was identified in the provided sources.
api_notes: The API covers repositories, issues, pull requests, users, and webhooks. It offers both REST and GraphQL interfaces.
access_notes: Developers can create GitHub accounts for free and generate personal access tokens or register GitHub Apps via the developer settings to access the API.
